panels.description: Distinct devices currently emitting telemetry. Counts unique ServiceName across one stable per-class gauge (compute: system.processes.count, network: system.network.interfaces.count, power: ups.battery.capacity). Tune thresholds to your fleet size.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
SELECT $timeSeries as t, uniqExact(ServiceName) as devices
FROM $table
WHERE $timeFilter
  AND MetricName IN (
    'system.processes.count',
    'system.network.interfaces.count',
    'ups.battery.capacity'
  )
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t
targets.rawQuery:
SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (60 * 1000)) * (60 * 1000)) as t, uniqExact(ServiceName) as devices
FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge
WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3)
  AND MetricName IN ('system.processes.count', 'system.network.interfaces.count', 'ups.battery.capacity')
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t

panels.title: Devices reporting

panels.description: Total receive throughput across all interfaces of all selected network devices. Sum of per-(device, interface) per-second rates derived from the cumulative ifHCInOctets counter (network.io / direction=receive).

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Total receive throughput = SUM over (device, interface) of per-series per-second rate.
--
-- Why not $perSecond(Value):
--   $perSecond does max(Value) per bucket across ALL series, then runningDifference.
--   With multiple (ServiceName, interface) series this collapses them into a single
--   pseudo-series and yields the rate of whichever interface had the largest counter.
--   We need per-series rate THEN sum, not the other way around.
--
-- Why three nesting levels:
--   ClickHouse cannot reference a window-function alias (prev_v) in the same SELECT,
--   so we compute lag in one layer, derive rate in the next, sum in the outermost.
--
-- Why the `if(prev_v > 0 AND v >= prev_v ...)` guard:
--   Cumulative counters reset on device reboot or counter wrap. Negative deltas would
--   show as huge negative throughput; we drop them (NULL) so they don't contribute.
--
-- Why `* 1000`:
--   $timeSeries returns the bucket boundary in milliseconds. (t - prev_t) is therefore
--   in ms; multiplying the byte delta by 1000 converts to bytes-per-second.
SELECT t, sum(rate_bps) as bytes_per_sec
FROM (
    SELECT t, v, prev_v, prev_t,
           if(prev_v > 0 AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t,
              (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t),
              NULL) as rate_bps
    FROM (
        SELECT t, series, v,
               lagInFrame(v) OVER w as prev_v,
               lagInFrame(t) OVER w as prev_t
        FROM (
            SELECT $timeSeries as t,
                   concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as series,
                   max(Value) as v
            FROM $table
            WHERE $timeFilter
              AND MetricName = 'network.io'
              AND Attributes['direction'] = 'receive'
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
              $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
            GROUP BY t, series
        )
        WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series ORDER BY t)
    )
)
WHERE rate_bps IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT t, sum(rate_bps) as bytes_per_sec FROM (SELECT t, v, prev_v, prev_t, if(prev_v > 0 AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t, (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t), NULL) as rate_bps FROM (SELECT t, series, v, lagInFrame(v) OVER w as prev_v, lagInFrame(t) OVER w as prev_t FROM (SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as series, max(Value) as v FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_sum WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'network.io' AND Attributes['direction'] = 'receive' GROUP BY t, series) WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series ORDER BY t))) WHERE rate_bps IS NOT NULL GROUP BY t ORDER BY t

panels.title: Total throughput out

panels.description: Interfaces that are unexpectedly down: oper_status != 1 (up) while admin_status == 1 (intentionally enabled). Excludes admin-down interfaces so planned shutdowns do not page on-call.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Count interfaces where admin_status = 1 (up) AND oper_status != 1 (not up).
--
-- Why pivot via anyLastIf instead of a JOIN:
--   admin_status and oper_status are two separate gauge metrics emitted on the same
--   (ServiceName, network.interface.name) cardinality. Pivoting both into columns of
--   one row per (bucket, series) is cheaper than self-joining the table.
--
-- Why anyLast within the bucket:
--   With $timeSeries bucketing, multiple samples may fall into one bucket. We want
--   the most recent reading per (series, metric), not max/min - status is categorical.
--
-- Why we do not filter on ServiceName via primary index:
--   We need to count across ALL selected network devices. The MetricName IN (2 values)
--   filter narrows enough at the 1-hour default range; bloom filter on Attributes is
--   not needed.
SELECT t, countIf(admin = 1 AND oper != 1) as ifaces_down
FROM (
    SELECT t, series,
           anyLastIf(v, mn = 'network.interface.admin_status') as admin,
           anyLastIf(v, mn = 'network.interface.oper_status')  as oper
    FROM (
        SELECT $timeSeries as t,
               concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as series,
               MetricName as mn,
               anyLast(Value) as v
        FROM $table
        WHERE $timeFilter
          AND MetricName IN ('network.interface.admin_status', 'network.interface.oper_status')
          $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
          $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
          $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
          $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
        GROUP BY t, series, mn
    )
    GROUP BY t, series
)
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT t, countIf(admin = 1 AND oper != 1) as ifaces_down FROM (SELECT t, series, anyLastIf(v, mn = 'network.interface.admin_status') as admin, anyLastIf(v, mn = 'network.interface.oper_status') as oper FROM (SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as series, MetricName as mn, anyLast(Value) as v FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName IN ('network.interface.admin_status', 'network.interface.oper_status') GROUP BY t, series, mn) GROUP BY t, series) GROUP BY t ORDER BY t

panels.title: Interfaces down

panels.description: Traffic light: red if ANY selected UPS reports ups.output.status = 3 (onBattery). PowerNet-MIB output status: 2=onLine, 3=onBattery, 4=onSmartBoost, 5=timedSleeping, 6=softwareBypass, 7=off, 8=rebooting, 9=switchedBypass, 10=hardwareFailureBypass, 11=sleepingUntilPowerReturn, 12=onSmartTrim.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Returns 1 if ANY selected UPS device has output status = 3 (onBattery), else 0.
--
-- Why anyLast and not max:
--   Status is categorical (2=onLine, 3=onBattery, 4=onSmartBoost, ...). max(Value)
--   would prefer larger codes regardless of recency. anyLast picks the most recent
--   sample per (bucket, device).
--
-- Why max(if(...)) at the outer level:
--   We are OR'ing across devices: if any UPS is on battery, the panel goes red.
--   max of a 0/1 indicator is the cheapest OR.
SELECT t, max(if(status = 3, 1, 0)) as on_battery
FROM (
    SELECT $timeSeries as t,
           ServiceName,
           anyLast(Value) as status
    FROM $table
    WHERE $timeFilter
      AND MetricName = 'ups.output.status'
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
      $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
    GROUP BY t, ServiceName
)
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT t, max(if(status = 3, 1, 0)) as on_battery FROM (SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, ServiceName, anyLast(Value) as status FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'ups.output.status' GROUP BY t, ServiceName) GROUP BY t ORDER BY t

panels.title: UPS on battery?

panels.description: Worst-case battery runtime in minutes - the smallest ups.battery.runtime_remaining across all selected UPS devices. PowerNet-MIB returns this in centiseconds (TimeTicks); we divide by 6000 to convert to minutes.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Shortest battery runtime among selected UPS devices, in minutes.
--
-- Why divide by 6000:
--   PowerNet-MIB upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining is a TimeTicks value in centiseconds
--   (1/100 second). Minutes = centiseconds / (100 * 60) = / 6000. A normalize step
--   in the Collector would remove this footgun, but for now the dashboard converts.
--
-- Why anyLast then min:
--   anyLast picks the most recent reading per (bucket, device); min across devices
--   surfaces the worst-case so on-call sees the most-at-risk UPS first.
--
-- Why no countIf > 0 guard:
--   If no UPS is selected, min() over zero rows returns NULL - Stat panel renders
--   'N/A' which is the correct UX (rather than '0 minutes' implying total failure).
SELECT t, min(runtime_minutes) as shortest_min
FROM (
    SELECT $timeSeries as t,
           ServiceName,
           anyLast(Value) / 6000 as runtime_minutes
    FROM $table
    WHERE $timeFilter
      AND MetricName = 'ups.battery.runtime_remaining'
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
      $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
    GROUP BY t, ServiceName
)
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT t, min(runtime_minutes) as shortest_min FROM (SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, ServiceName, anyLast(Value) / 6000 as runtime_minutes FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'ups.battery.runtime_remaining' GROUP BY t, ServiceName) GROUP BY t ORDER BY t

panels.title: Shortest UPS runtime

panels.description: Per-interface throughput, stacked area. Two series per interface - one for receive, one for transmit. Series labeled 'iface (direction)' - disambiguated with device prefix when multiple devices share an interface name.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Per-interface throughput. Same per-series rate machinery as tiles 'Total
-- throughput in/out' (id=2,3); see those panels for the rationale on lagInFrame,
-- the three nesting levels, the counter-reset guard, and the * 1000 ms->s conversion.
--
-- Why we emit (t, label, value) instead of summing:
--   The header tiles want a single number - they sum across series. This panel wants
--   one line per (interface, direction). Vertamedia auto-pivots (t, label, value)
--   into separate Grafana series; we just have to keep the label column.
--
-- Why label = '<iface> (<dir>)' with optional device prefix:
--   In a single-site / single-device deployment, just 'eth0 (rx)' is enough. When
--   multiple devices share an interface name (likely as the fleet grows), countDistinct
--   triggers the device-prefix branch so series stay unambiguous.
--
-- Why ORDER BY label, t (not just t):
--   Vertamedia's time_series mode requires rows of the same series to be contiguous so
--   the plugin can split them. Sorting by (label, t) puts each series in one block.
SELECT t, label, rate_bps as value
FROM (
    SELECT t, label, v, prev_v, prev_t,
           if(prev_v > 0 AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t,
              (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t),
              NULL) as rate_bps
    FROM (
        SELECT t, label, v,
               lagInFrame(v) OVER w as prev_v,
               lagInFrame(t) OVER w as prev_t
        FROM (
            SELECT $timeSeries as t,
                   if(
                       (SELECT countDistinct(ServiceName) FROM $table
                        WHERE $timeFilter AND MetricName = 'network.io') > 1,
                       concat(ServiceName, '·', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], ' (', Attributes['direction'], ')'),
                       concat(ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], ' (', Attributes['direction'], ')')
                   ) as label,
                   concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], '|', Attributes['direction']) as series_key,
                   max(Value) as v
            FROM $table
            WHERE $timeFilter
              AND MetricName = 'network.io'
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
              $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
            GROUP BY t, label, series_key
        )
        WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series_key ORDER BY t)
    )
)
WHERE rate_bps IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY label, t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT t, label, rate_bps as value FROM (SELECT t, label, v, prev_v, prev_t, if(prev_v > 0 AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t, (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t), NULL) as rate_bps FROM (SELECT t, label, v, lagInFrame(v) OVER w as prev_v, lagInFrame(t) OVER w as prev_t FROM (SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], ' (', Attributes['direction'], ')') as label, concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], '|', Attributes['direction']) as series_key, max(Value) as v FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_sum WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'network.io' GROUP BY t, label, series_key) WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series_key ORDER BY t))) WHERE rate_bps IS NOT NULL ORDER BY label, t

panels.title: Per-interface throughput

panels.description: Top 10 (device, interface) pairs by total throughput over the selected time range. Receive and transmit averages broken out, ranked by their sum. Scales with fleet - when more devices are selected, the table still shows only the busiest 10.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Top 10 interfaces by total throughput across the selected time range.
--
-- Same per-series rate machinery as panel 'Per-interface throughput' (id=7); see
-- tile 'Total throughput in' (id=2) for the full rationale on lagInFrame, the three
-- nesting levels, the counter-reset guard, and the * 1000 ms->s conversion.
--
-- Why we then pivot direction into rx/tx columns:
--   The per-series rate gives one row per (t, device, iface, dir). For a top-N table
--   we want one row per (device, iface) - averaged across time, with rx and tx as
--   columns so the operator can see asymmetric flows at a glance.
--
-- Why ORDER BY (Avg_rx + Avg_tx) DESC, LIMIT 10:
--   Total bidirectional traffic is the most useful single ranking. ClickHouse cannot
--   reference SELECT aliases in ORDER BY in some plans, so we re-state the sum.
--
-- format='table': panel renders one Grafana column per SELECT column.
SELECT
    label as Interface,
    avgIf(rate_bps, dir = 'receive')  as `Avg ↓`,
    avgIf(rate_bps, dir = 'transmit') as `Avg ↑`,
    (avgIf(rate_bps, dir = 'receive') + avgIf(rate_bps, dir = 'transmit')) as Total
FROM (
    SELECT label, dir, rate_bps
    FROM (
        SELECT t, label, dir, v, prev_v, prev_t,
               if(prev_v > 0 AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t,
                  (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t),
                  NULL) as rate_bps
        FROM (
            SELECT t, label, dir, v,
                   lagInFrame(v) OVER w as prev_v,
                   lagInFrame(t) OVER w as prev_t
            FROM (
                SELECT $timeSeries as t,
                       concat(ServiceName, '·', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as label,
                       Attributes['direction'] as dir,
                       concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], '|', Attributes['direction']) as series_key,
                       max(Value) as v
                FROM $table
                WHERE $timeFilter
                  AND MetricName = 'network.io'
                  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
                  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
                  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
                  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
                GROUP BY t, label, dir, series_key
            )
            WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series_key ORDER BY t)
        )
        WHERE rate_bps IS NOT NULL
    )
)
GROUP BY label
ORDER BY Total DESC
LIMIT 10
targets.rawQuery: SELECT label as Interface, avgIf(rate_bps, dir = 'receive') as `Avg ↓`, avgIf(rate_bps, dir = 'transmit') as `Avg ↑`, (avgIf(rate_bps, dir = 'receive') + avgIf(rate_bps, dir = 'transmit')) as Total FROM (SELECT label, dir, rate_bps FROM (SELECT t, label, dir, v, prev_v, prev_t, if(prev_v > 0 AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t, (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t), NULL) as rate_bps FROM (SELECT t, label, dir, v, lagInFrame(v) OVER w as prev_v, lagInFrame(t) OVER w as prev_t FROM (SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ServiceName, '·', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as label, Attributes['direction'] as dir, concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], '|', Attributes['direction']) as series_key, max(Value) as v FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_sum WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'network.io' GROUP BY t, label, dir, series_key) WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series_key ORDER BY t)) WHERE rate_bps IS NOT NULL)) GROUP BY label ORDER BY Total DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top 10 interfaces by throughput

panels.description: Distribution of per-(interface, direction) error rates over time. Each cell shows how many series fell into a given errors-per-second bucket at that moment. Spec stops: 1/s (warning) and 10/s (critical). Receive and transmit kept as separate series - RX errors point upstream/cabling, TX errors point at the local NIC/queue.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Per-(device, interface, direction) error rate, fed to a Grafana heatmap so cells
-- represent 'how many series had this error rate at this time'.
--
-- Why toNullable(v) and toNullable(t) inside lagInFrame:
--   lagInFrame returns the type's default (0 for numeric) when there is no preceding
--   row, which is indistinguishable from an actual previous reading of 0. For
--   throughput counters that start in the millions this rarely matters; for ERROR
--   counters that hover at 0 it produces a fake huge spike at the first sample.
--   Wrapping in toNullable() makes the absent case truly NULL so the if() guard skips
--   the first row of each series cleanly without dropping legitimate zeros.
--
-- Why the rate guard differs from the throughput panels:
--   We accept prev_v >= 0 (NOT > 0) because zero-error baselines are meaningful - a
--   long stretch of zero is exactly what we want to see at the bottom of the heatmap.
--   Filtering them out would hide the healthy state.
--
-- Why direction is in the series_key but not in the heatmap calculation:
--   The heatmap collapses series into bucket counts. Including direction in series_key
--   prevents rx and tx from sharing a lag frame (they are independent counters and
--   would distort each other's deltas). The label is informational for the tooltip.
SELECT t, label, rate_eps as value
FROM (
    SELECT t, label, v, prev_v, prev_t,
           if(prev_v IS NOT NULL AND prev_t IS NOT NULL AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t,
              (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t),
              NULL) as rate_eps
    FROM (
        SELECT t, label, v,
               lagInFrame(toNullable(v)) OVER w as prev_v,
               lagInFrame(toNullable(t)) OVER w as prev_t
        FROM (
            SELECT $timeSeries as t,
                   concat(ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], ' (', Attributes['direction'], ')') as label,
                   concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], '|', Attributes['direction']) as series_key,
                   max(Value) as v
            FROM $table
            WHERE $timeFilter
              AND MetricName = 'network.errors'
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
              $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
              $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
            GROUP BY t, label, series_key
        )
        WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series_key ORDER BY t)
    )
)
WHERE rate_eps IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY label, t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT t, label, rate_eps as value FROM (SELECT t, label, v, prev_v, prev_t, if(prev_v IS NOT NULL AND prev_t IS NOT NULL AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t, (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t), NULL) as rate_eps FROM (SELECT t, label, v, lagInFrame(toNullable(v)) OVER w as prev_v, lagInFrame(toNullable(t)) OVER w as prev_t FROM (SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], ' (', Attributes['direction'], ')') as label, concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], '|', Attributes['direction']) as series_key, max(Value) as v FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_sum WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'network.errors' GROUP BY t, label, series_key) WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series_key ORDER BY t))) WHERE rate_eps IS NOT NULL ORDER BY label, t

panels.title: Interface error rate

panels.description: Admin and operational status per interface as a state timeline. One swim lane per (device, interface, status type). On-call eyes pick up colour blocks faster than 0/1 lines: green = up (1), red = down (2), yellow = testing/unknown/dormant (3/4/5).

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Per-interface admin and oper status as state-timeline swim lanes.
--
-- Why anyLast and not max:
--   Status values are categorical (1=up, 2=down, 3=testing, 4=unknown, 5=dormant).
--   max() would prefer larger codes regardless of recency. anyLast picks the most
--   recent reading per (bucket, series) which is what 'current state' means.
--
-- Why label always includes ServiceName:
--   Different devices can have identically-named interfaces (e.g. eth0 on every Linux
--   host). Without the device prefix, lanes would collide. The verbose label is the
--   cost of correctness.
--
-- Why mergeValues = true (panel option, not SQL):
--   The Vertamedia plugin emits one row per 30s bucket. Without merging, the timeline
--   draws a separate rectangle per bucket. mergeValues = true coalesces consecutive
--   equal values into one block, which is what makes the 'colour block' UX work.
--
-- Why thresholds AND mappings:
--   Mappings give the human-readable text in the tooltip and legend ("down" not 2).
--   Thresholds drive the color of unmapped values (e.g., a future code 6) so the
--   panel never falls back to the default grey.
SELECT $timeSeries as t,
       concat(
           ServiceName, ' · ',
           ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], ' (',
           if(MetricName = 'network.interface.admin_status', 'admin', 'oper'),
           ')'
       ) as label,
       anyLast(Value) as value
FROM $table
WHERE $timeFilter
  AND MetricName IN ('network.interface.admin_status', 'network.interface.oper_status')
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
GROUP BY t, label
ORDER BY label, t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ServiceName, ' · ', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], ' (', if(MetricName = 'network.interface.admin_status', 'admin', 'oper'), ')') as label, anyLast(Value) as value FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName IN ('network.interface.admin_status', 'network.interface.oper_status') GROUP BY t, label ORDER BY label, t

panels.title: Admin / Oper status

panels.description: One gauge per interface showing utilization as a percentage of nominal link speed, computed as max(rx_util, tx_util) since full-duplex links treat directions independently. Warn at 70%, crit at 90%.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- One gauge per interface, showing peak directional utilization as % of nominal speed.
--
-- Why argMax/argMin instead of lagInFrame:
--   Other rate panels need per-bucket time-series; this one needs a single number per
--   interface for the entire dashboard time range. argMax(Value, TimeUnix) and the
--   matching argMin pick the latest and earliest counter readings inside the window;
--   their delta divided by the actual elapsed seconds is the average rate over that
--   window. No window function needed - saves three subquery layers.
--
-- Why JOIN sum + gauge tables instead of one query:
--   network.io is a monotonic counter (otel_metrics_sum); network.interface.speed is
--   a point-in-time gauge (otel_metrics_gauge). They live in different tables; a CTE
--   per source plus an ANY LEFT JOIN on the (device, interface) key combines them.
--
-- Why max(rx_util, tx_util) per interface:
--   Full-duplex Ethernet treats receive and transmit as independent half-channels of
--   equal capacity. The link is 'saturated' when EITHER direction approaches the
--   nominal speed. Surfacing the busier of the two matches operator intuition.
--
-- Why nullIf(speed_mbps * 1e6, 0):
--   Some virtual interfaces (lo, tun) report ifHighSpeed = 0. Without the guard the
--   division would produce inf and the gauge would render as 'No data'.
WITH
  io_window AS (
    SELECT
        concat(ServiceName, ' · ', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as iface_label,
        Attributes['direction'] as dir,
        argMax(Value, TimeUnix) as v_end,
        argMin(Value, TimeUnix) as v_start,
        dateDiff('second', min(TimeUnix), max(TimeUnix)) as window_s
    FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_sum
    WHERE $timeFilter
      AND MetricName = 'network.io'
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
      $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
    GROUP BY iface_label, dir
  ),
  rates AS (
    SELECT iface_label, dir,
           if(v_end >= v_start AND window_s > 0,
              (v_end - v_start) / window_s,
              0) as rate_bps
    FROM io_window
  ),
  speeds AS (
    SELECT
        concat(ServiceName, ' · ', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as iface_label,
        anyLast(Value) as speed_mbps
    FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge
    WHERE $timeFilter
      AND MetricName = 'network.interface.speed'
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
      $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
    GROUP BY iface_label
  )
SELECT
    r.iface_label as Interface,
    max((r.rate_bps * 8) / nullIf(s.speed_mbps * 1e6, 0) * 100) as `Util %`
FROM rates r
ANY LEFT JOIN speeds s ON r.iface_label = s.iface_label
GROUP BY r.iface_label
ORDER BY `Util %` DESC
targets.rawQuery: WITH io_window AS (SELECT concat(ServiceName, ' · ', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as iface_label, Attributes['direction'] as dir, argMax(Value, TimeUnix) as v_end, argMin(Value, TimeUnix) as v_start, dateDiff('second', min(TimeUnix), max(TimeUnix)) as window_s FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_sum WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'network.io' GROUP BY iface_label, dir), rates AS (SELECT iface_label, dir, if(v_end >= v_start AND window_s > 0, (v_end - v_start) / window_s, 0) as rate_bps FROM io_window), speeds AS (SELECT concat(ServiceName, ' · ', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name']) as iface_label, anyLast(Value) as speed_mbps FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'network.interface.speed' GROUP BY iface_label) SELECT r.iface_label as Interface, max((r.rate_bps * 8) / nullIf(s.speed_mbps * 1e6, 0) * 100) as `Util %` FROM rates r ANY LEFT JOIN speeds s ON r.iface_label = s.iface_label GROUP BY r.iface_label ORDER BY `Util %` DESC

panels.title: Link utilization %

panels.description: Number of interfaces reported by each network device (IF-MIB ifNumber, surfaced as system.network.interfaces.count). Informational - a sudden change usually means a config push or hardware swap, not a fault.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- One stat tile per network device showing the latest interface count.
--
-- Why format='table' + values=true:
--   The Stat panel renders one tile per row when values=true. table format gives us
--   exactly one row per ServiceName, which becomes one tile labeled by Device.
--
-- Why anyLast and not max:
--   The interface count is a point-in-time inventory number; the most recent reading
--   is what we want, not the historical peak. anyLast picks the latest sample per
--   ServiceName within the dashboard time range.
SELECT ServiceName as Device,
       anyLast(Value) as Interfaces
FROM $table
WHERE $timeFilter
  AND MetricName = 'system.network.interfaces.count'
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
GROUP BY ServiceName
ORDER BY ServiceName
targets.rawQuery: SELECT ServiceName as Device, anyLast(Value) as Interfaces FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'system.network.interfaces.count' GROUP BY ServiceName ORDER BY ServiceName

panels.title: Interface count per device

panels.description: Per-core CPU utilization for each compute device. Latest reading from HOST-RESOURCES hrProcessorLoad. Warn at 80%, crit at 95%. Label format: '<device> · CPU <oid-suffix>' - the suffix is the SNMP table index, not the kernel core number.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- One gauge per (device, CPU core) showing the latest utilization sample.
--
-- Why replaceOne(cpu.index, 'cpu_.', 'CPU '):
--   The snmpreceiver uses indexed_value_prefix='cpu_' for hrProcessorTable, which
--   prepends literally to '.<oid-suffix>' giving values like 'cpu_.196608'. Strip the
--   prefix and the dot, label as 'CPU <suffix>'. This is the workaround documented in
--   DASHBOARD.md 'Open questions' until a transform processor is added to the
--   collector.
--
-- Why anyLast(Value):
--   This panel shows current per-core load. anyLast picks the most recent reading,
--   which is what a gauge representing 'right now' should show. avg() would smooth
--   away the very spikes the operator wants to see.
SELECT
    concat(ServiceName, ' · ', replaceOne(ResourceAttributes['cpu.index'], 'cpu_.', 'CPU ')) as Core,
    anyLast(Value) as `Util %`
FROM $table
WHERE $timeFilter
  AND MetricName = 'system.cpu.utilization'
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
GROUP BY Core
ORDER BY Core
targets.rawQuery: SELECT concat(ServiceName, ' · ', replaceOne(ResourceAttributes['cpu.index'], 'cpu_.', 'CPU ')) as Core, anyLast(Value) as `Util %` FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'system.cpu.utilization' GROUP BY Core ORDER BY Core

panels.title: CPU utilization per core

panels.description: Three load-average lines per compute device: 1-minute, 5-minute, 15-minute. UCD-SNMP-MIB laLoadInt returns each value pre-multiplied by 100 (so a load of 1.42 arrives as 142); we divide by 100 to display the conventional fractional load.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Three load-average lines per device (1m, 5m, 15m).
--
-- Why divide by 100:
--   UCD-SNMP-MIB exposes laLoadInt.1/.2/.3 as integers pre-scaled by 100, because
--   SNMP Integer32 has no fractional support. The Collector emits the raw scaled
--   values and documents the scaling in each metric description; the dashboard does
--   the divide so the legend shows '0.42' not '42'.
--
-- Why nested if() instead of replaceOne or substring:
--   Three discrete metric names map to three short labels. A nested if() is the
--   clearest, branch-free formulation; a regex or substring would be both slower and
--   more fragile if a fourth load metric is ever added.
--
-- Why avg(Value):
--   Multiple samples may fall in one $timeSeries bucket. Load average is already a
--   smoothed value, so avg() across the bucket is meaningful (and stable). anyLast
--   would be defensible too - pick whichever, but be consistent across panels.
SELECT $timeSeries as t,
       concat(
           ServiceName, ' · ',
           if(MetricName = 'system.cpu.load_average.1m',  '1m',
           if(MetricName = 'system.cpu.load_average.5m',  '5m', '15m'))
       ) as label,
       avg(Value) / 100 as value
FROM $table
WHERE $timeFilter
  AND MetricName IN (
    'system.cpu.load_average.1m',
    'system.cpu.load_average.5m',
    'system.cpu.load_average.15m'
  )
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
GROUP BY t, label
ORDER BY label, t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ServiceName, ' · ', if(MetricName = 'system.cpu.load_average.1m', '1m', if(MetricName = 'system.cpu.load_average.5m', '5m', '15m'))) as label, avg(Value) / 100 as value FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName IN ('system.cpu.load_average.1m', 'system.cpu.load_average.5m', 'system.cpu.load_average.15m') GROUP BY t, label ORDER BY label, t

panels.title: Load average (1m / 5m / 15m)

panels.description: Percent of physical memory in use per compute device, computed from UCD memTotalReal and memAvailReal: 100 * (1 - available / total). Warn at 85%, crit at 95%.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- One stat tile per compute device showing current memory utilization.
--
-- Why pivot via anyLastIf instead of two queries:
--   total and available are two gauges keyed by ServiceName. anyLastIf(value, cond)
--   pulls the latest matching reading for each metric in a single pass over the table.
--   Cheaper than running two SELECTs and joining client-side.
--
-- Why nullIf(total, 0):
--   memTotalReal can briefly be 0 immediately after device boot or during snmpsim
--   restart. Without the guard, division produces NaN/inf and the stat renders as
--   'No data' instead of falling through to the next bucket.
--
-- Why 100 * (1 - avail / total) rather than (total - avail) / total * 100:
--   Algebraically identical, but the form '1 - avail/total' is one fewer subtraction
--   and matches how the metric is conceptually defined ('what fraction is NOT free').
SELECT
    ServiceName as Device,
    100.0 * (1 - avail / nullIf(total, 0)) as `Used %`
FROM (
    SELECT
        ServiceName,
        anyLastIf(Value, MetricName = 'system.memory.total')     as total,
        anyLastIf(Value, MetricName = 'system.memory.available') as avail
    FROM $table
    WHERE $timeFilter
      AND MetricName IN ('system.memory.total', 'system.memory.available')
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
      $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
      $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
    GROUP BY ServiceName
)
WHERE total IS NOT NULL AND avail IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY Device
targets.rawQuery: SELECT ServiceName as Device, 100.0 * (1 - avail / nullIf(total, 0)) as `Used %` FROM (SELECT ServiceName, anyLastIf(Value, MetricName = 'system.memory.total') as total, anyLastIf(Value, MetricName = 'system.memory.available') as avail FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName IN ('system.memory.total', 'system.memory.available') GROUP BY ServiceName) WHERE total IS NOT NULL AND avail IS NOT NULL ORDER BY Device

panels.title: Memory used %

panels.description: Stacked breakdown of memory by class - cached, buffered, available - per compute device. Useful for spotting leaks: a slow climb in 'used' coupled with shrinking 'available' that buffers/cache cannot reclaim is the classic shape.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Three stacked memory series per device: cached, buffered, available.
--
-- Why multiply by 1024:
--   The Collector emits these gauges in KiBy (UCD memory metrics are kibibytes). The
--   Grafana 'bytes' unit expects raw bytes and auto-formats to MiB/GiB at render time.
--   Multiplying once at the SQL boundary keeps every panel reading the same metric in
--   the same unit, so legends and Y-axes line up across panels.
--
-- Why nested if() rather than a CASE / multi-step pivot:
--   Three discrete metric names, three short labels - same pattern as the load-average
--   panel (id=14). Branch-free and easy to extend if 'free' or 'shared' is ever added.
--
-- Why label always includes ServiceName:
--   With multiple compute devices selected, the stacked areas need to be unambiguous.
--   Cost is a verbose legend; benefit is no silent lane collisions.
SELECT $timeSeries as t,
       concat(
           ServiceName, ' · ',
           if(MetricName = 'system.memory.cached',    'cached',
           if(MetricName = 'system.memory.buffered',  'buffered', 'available'))
       ) as label,
       avg(Value) * 1024 as value
FROM $table
WHERE $timeFilter
  AND MetricName IN (
    'system.memory.cached',
    'system.memory.buffered',
    'system.memory.available'
  )
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
GROUP BY t, label
ORDER BY label, t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ServiceName, ' · ', if(MetricName = 'system.memory.cached', 'cached', if(MetricName = 'system.memory.buffered', 'buffered', 'available'))) as label, avg(Value) * 1024 as value FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_gauge WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName IN ('system.memory.cached', 'system.memory.buffered', 'system.memory.available') GROUP BY t, label ORDER BY label, t

panels.title: UPS output status

panels.description: Total interface errors per second, summed across all selected (device, interface, direction) series. Spike here that aligns with a UPS state change above is the smoking gun.

targets.formattedQuery: SELECT $timeSeries as t, count() FROM $table WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY t ORDER BY t
targets.query:
-- Aggregate interface error rate (sum of per-series per-second rates) across
-- the entire selected fleet. Same lagInFrame machinery as tile 'Total throughput
-- in' (id=2); see that panel for the rationale on the three nesting levels, the
-- counter-reset guard, and the * 1000 ms->s conversion. Includes both rx and tx.
SELECT t, sum(rate_eps) as `errors/s`
FROM (
    SELECT t, v, prev_v, prev_t,
           if(prev_v IS NOT NULL AND prev_t IS NOT NULL AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t,
              (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t),
              NULL) as rate_eps
    FROM (
        SELECT t, series, v,
               lagInFrame(toNullable(v)) OVER w as prev_v,
               lagInFrame(toNullable(t)) OVER w as prev_t
        FROM (
            SELECT $timeSeries as t,
                   concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], '|', Attributes['direction']) as series,
                   max(Value) as v
            FROM $table
            WHERE $timeFilter
              AND MetricName = 'network.errors'
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
            GROUP BY t, series
        )
        WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series ORDER BY t)
    )
)
WHERE rate_eps IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t
targets.rawQuery: SELECT t, sum(rate_eps) as `errors/s` FROM (SELECT t, v, prev_v, prev_t, if(prev_v IS NOT NULL AND prev_t IS NOT NULL AND v >= prev_v AND t > prev_t, (v - prev_v) * 1000 / (t - prev_t), NULL) as rate_eps FROM (SELECT t, series, v, lagInFrame(toNullable(v)) OVER w as prev_v, lagInFrame(toNullable(t)) OVER w as prev_t FROM (SELECT (intDiv(toFloat64(TimeUnix) * 1000, (30 * 1000)) * (30 * 1000)) as t, concat(ServiceName, '|', ResourceAttributes['network.interface.name'], '|', Attributes['direction']) as series, max(Value) as v FROM infraspec.otel_metrics_sum WHERE TimeUnix >= toDateTime64(now() - 3600, 3) AND TimeUnix <= toDateTime64(now(), 3) AND MetricName = 'network.errors' GROUP BY t, series) WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY series ORDER BY t))) WHERE rate_eps IS NOT NULL GROUP BY t ORDER BY t

targets.query:
-- Fleet-average available memory in bytes. Source unit is KiBy (UCD memAvailReal),

SELECT $timeSeries as t,
       avg(Value) * 1024 as `avg memory available`
FROM $table
WHERE $timeFilter
  AND MetricName = 'system.memory.available'
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['environment'] IN (${environment:singlequote}), $environment)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['site.id']     IN (${site:singlequote}),         $site)
  $conditionalTest(AND ResourceAttributes['device.kind'] IN (${device_kind:singlequote}),  $device_kind)
  $conditionalTest(AND ServiceName                       IN (${device:singlequote}),       $device)
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t
